rulings.Case Title: 367 CHURCH STREET HOA VS. SHANE DUNNE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Verified Petition For Order Confirming Arbitration Award And For Judgment In Conformance Therewith
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for March 26, 2025, line 1. PETITIONER 367 CHURCH STREET HOA'S Verified Petition For Order Confirming Arbitration Award And For Judgment In Conformance Therewith. Notice of Hearing on Verified Petition for Order Confirming Arbitration Award and for Judgment in Conformance Therewith is GRANTED. No opposition filed. The Court notes that the Register of Actions reflects 4 filings made by the respondent on March 12, 13, and 20, 2025. The filings are not accompanied by proofs of service and are not set for hearing. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MHYF HOLDINGS, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. CHRIS V. KELLY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for March 26, 2025, line 4. DEFENDANTS CHRIS KELLY INDIVIDUALLY AND AS CO-TRUSTEE, LARA KELLY'S DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT. Defendants Kellys' Demurrer to Amended Complaint is SUSTAINED. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend from Plaintiff to allege fraud and non-disclosure causes of action with requisite particularity as to each representation/concealment/failure to disclose connecting them to reliance and resulting damages. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action for breach of covenant of good faith and fair dealing. Contrary to Plaintiff's statement made on March 3, 2025 in opposition, there is no dismissal on file as of the time of this ruling and there was no substantive opposition to the demurrer. Demurrer as to the cause of action for professional negligence is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiffs to allege facts giving rise to liability. The Court notes that opposition contained no cases factually similar to this action holding the owners of the property vicariously liable for hired engineer's professional negligence. Demurrer to claims brought individually by Humphrey and Fu is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts supporting standing. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SF 701 TAYLOR ST, LLC VS. AMY PHILLIPS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Enforcement Of Settlement Agreement, Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Ccp Section 664.6 And Substitution Of Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion calendar for March 26, 2025, line 6. PLAINTIFF SF OWNER LLC'S Motion For Enforcement Of Settlement Agreement, Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To CCP Section 664.6 And Substitution Of Plaintiff. Plaintiff's Motion for Enforcement of Settlement Agreement, Entry of Judgment and Substitution of Plaintiff is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. SF Owner LLC is substituted as the plaintiff. Motion for entry of judgment is denied. Moving party has not offered any competent evidence showing how or when Defendant was provided with the "takeover notice" which purportedly informed her of the new instructions for payment. =(501/HLJ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
